findings.description: Uses UserInfo.getSessionId() which is banned as of Spring '26 enforcement (Feb 16, 2026). Session IDs have been removed from outbound messages and are being phased out across the platform. This is the ONLY instance of getSessionId() in the entire codebase. ToolingApiService is used by HealthCheckScanner, SessionSettingsScanner, MFAComplianceScanner, and AuditTrailScanner - making this a high-blast-radius issue.
findings.recommendation: Replace UserInfo.getSessionId() with a Named Credential or Connected App OAuth flow. Configure a Named Credential targeting the org's own Tooling API endpoint and use 'callout:ElaroTooling' as the endpoint prefix. This is the only file requiring this change.

findings.description: The @AuraEnabled method executeNaturalLanguageQuery catches Exception but exposes raw exception messages to the client via result.naturalLanguageSummary = 'Error executing query: ' + e.getMessage(). This can leak internal SOQL syntax errors, object/field names, or permission details to the LWC frontend. The method does not throw AuraHandledException.
findings.recommendation: Replace e.getMessage() with a generic error message: result.naturalLanguageSummary = 'An error occurred processing your query. Please try again.' The detailed error is already logged via ElaroLogger.error on line 89.

findings.description: GUEST-ACCESSIBLE controller's @AuraEnabled method getPublicData catches Exception and returns a generic error in the response wrapper but does NOT throw AuraHandledException. Without AuraHandledException, the LWC imperative call's .catch() handler won't trigger, potentially leaving the UI in an inconsistent state. The error message itself is properly generic.
findings.recommendation: After setting response.errorMessage, add: throw new AuraHandledException(response.errorMessage); Or restructure to throw AuraHandledException directly in the catch block.

findings.description: The @AuraEnabled method evaluateAndPublish catches Exception but exposes raw exception message: out.message = 'Error evaluating performance: ' + e.getMessage(). Returns the result object instead of throwing AuraHandledException, leaking internal error details to the LWC client.
findings.recommendation: Throw new AuraHandledException('Unable to evaluate performance metrics. Please verify permissions and try again.') instead of returning the error in the result object. The full error is already logged via ElaroLogger.error on line 80.

findings.description: The @AuraEnabled(cacheable=true) method getRealtimeStats catches Exception with ElaroLogger.warn but silently returns default values without throwing AuraHandledException. The LWC @wire error handler never fires, so the UI displays zeros without indicating an error occurred. This can mask permission issues or org configuration problems.
findings.recommendation: Replace the catch block to throw new AuraHandledException('Unable to retrieve real-time statistics. Please verify you have the required permissions and try again.') so the LWC error state renders.

findings.description: The @AuraEnabled(cacheable=true) method isShieldEncryptionEnabled catches Exception but returns false instead of throwing AuraHandledException. While the method has try-catch and ElaroLogger.warn, returning false on error is a false negative that could lead to incorrect PCI compliance status being displayed.
findings.recommendation: Consider throwing AuraHandledException so the LWC can show an error state rather than falsely indicating Shield is not enabled. Alternatively, return a wrapper object with an error flag.

findings.description: Uses 'without sharing' with a justification comment ('Runs in system context to poll audit trail regardless of user permissions') but does not follow the standard SECURITY: prefix format used consistently by other without-sharing classes in the codebase (RuleEngineEventBridge, ComplianceAlertPublisher, ElaroEventPublisher, SchedulerErrorHandler).
findings.recommendation: Update the ApexDoc to: '* SECURITY: without sharing required because this scheduled job polls SetupAuditTrail entries and publishes Platform Events for audit processing, which must succeed regardless of the running user\'s record access.'

findings.description: Uses Database.query() with string concatenation for field/object names from Custom Metadata. Mitigations are in place: (1) String.escapeSingleQuotes on both field and object names, (2) AccessLevel.USER_MODE, (3) NOPMD suppression comment documenting the rationale. Inputs come from admin-controlled Custom Metadata, not user input.
findings.recommendation: For additional hardening, validate settingName and fieldName match expected patterns (regex: ^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*(__c)?$) before query construction. Current mitigations are acceptable for Custom Metadata inputs.

findings.description: Executes the entire SOQL query string from Compliance_Rule__mdt.Evaluation_Query__c via Database.query(). While Custom Metadata is admin-controlled and AccessLevel.USER_MODE is used, the full query is a stored string with no structural validation. A compromised admin Custom Metadata record could query any accessible object.
findings.recommendation: Add an object allowlist validation (similar to NaturalLanguageQueryService.isSOQLSafe()) before execution to ensure queries only target expected compliance objects. The NOPMD comment and AccessLevel.USER_MODE are good existing mitigations.

findings.description: Pure DTO class missing explicit sharing keyword declaration. Contains no SOQL or DML. Salesforce Code Analyzer may flag this. Without an explicit keyword, the class defaults to the caller's sharing context, which is typically fine for DTOs but inconsistent with project standards.
findings.recommendation: Add 'inherited sharing' to the class declaration: 'public inherited sharing class ScanRecommendation implements Comparable'

findings.description: Pure DTO class missing explicit sharing keyword declaration. Contains no SOQL or DML.
findings.recommendation: Add 'inherited sharing': 'public inherited sharing class ScanFinding'

findings.description: Pure DTO class missing explicit sharing keyword declaration. Contains no SOQL or DML.
findings.recommendation: Add 'inherited sharing': 'public inherited sharing class HealthCheckResult'

findings.description: REST API callback endpoint demonstrates exemplary defense-in-depth: API key authentication with SHA256 hashing, replay protection (timestamp + nonce validation), HMAC-SHA256 signature validation with constant-time comparison (prevents timing attacks), rate limiting via Platform Cache, correlation IDs for audit logging, sanitized error responses (no stack traces exposed), and CRUD/FLS enforcement via Security.stripInaccessible + AccessLevel.USER_MODE.
findings.recommendation: Use as reference implementation for future REST API endpoints.

findings.description: Dynamic report builder demonstrates exemplary security: object allowlisting (ALLOWED_OBJECTS), operator allowlisting (ALLOWED_OPERATORS), field validation against Schema.getGlobalDescribe(), bind variables via Database.queryWithBinds(), Security.stripInaccessible for FLS, WITH USER_MODE, relationship depth limiting (max 5), sanitizeFieldName() removing non-alphanumeric characters, and proper AuraHandledException on all methods.
findings.recommendation: Use as reference implementation for dynamic query builders.

findings.description: Without-sharing class with the most thorough security justification in the codebase: 4 specific reasons for without sharing, 4 security mitigations documented. Includes HTML/XSS escaping via escapeHtml(), deterministic hashing for audit trail integrity, and correlation IDs for traceability.
findings.recommendation: The ApexDoc format here should be adopted as the standard for without-sharing justifications across the codebase.

findings.description: AI-powered SOQL generation implements defense-in-depth: object allowlisting (ALLOWED_OBJECTS), DML keyword blocking in isSOQLSafe(), LIMIT enforcement, WITH USER_MODE injection via addSecurityEnforced(), Database.queryWithBinds with AccessLevel.USER_MODE, Security.stripInaccessible before returning to UI, and proper AuraHandledException on getQuerySuggestions/validateQuery/getRecentQueries.
findings.recommendation: Good multi-layer defense pattern. The executeNaturalLanguageQuery method should align its error handling with the other methods (SEC-002).

findings.description: Controller properly re-throws AuraHandledException while catching and wrapping all other exceptions. Feature flag check before expensive operation. Structured logging with HCLogger. Uses 'with sharing'.
findings.recommendation: Good reference pattern for controller-level error handling.

findings.description: Guest-accessible controller with multi-layered security: token validation, link expiration checking, access recording for audit trail, Is_Public__c filter on all queries, WITH USER_MODE, and LIMIT 20. Only exposes Trust_Center_View__c (public-safe object). Comprehensive security documentation listing 6 absolute requirements for guest controllers.
findings.recommendation: Use as reference for future guest-accessible controllers. Fix error handling per SEC-003.

findings.description: All 5 findings from Feb 2026 review (SEC-001 through SEC-005) have been remediated. The class now uses 'insert as user', 'update as user', 'delete as user', and all @AuraEnabled methods have proper try-catch with ElaroLogger.error() and AuraHandledException.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Remediation verified.

findings.description: All 9 findings from Feb 2026 review (SEC-006 through SEC-014) have been remediated. The class now uses 'as user' for all DML, WITH USER_MODE for all SOQL, try-catch with AuraHandledException on all @AuraEnabled methods, and the @future method has been removed.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Remediation verified.

findings.description: All 6 findings from Feb 2026 review (SEC-020 through SEC-025) have been remediated. All @AuraEnabled methods now have try-catch with ElaroLogger.error() and AuraHandledException. All DML uses 'as user'.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Remediation verified.

findings.description: The Feb 2026 review (SEC-018) flagged Database.query() usage. This has been remediated to use Database.queryWithBinds(qwb.query, qwb.binds, AccessLevel.USER_MODE) with a structured QueryWithBinds wrapper class.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Remediation verified.

findings.description: The Feb 2026 review (SEC-015) flagged Database.query() usage. This has been remediated to use Database.queryWithBinds(secureSOQL, binds, AccessLevel.USER_MODE).
findings.recommendation: No action required. Remediation verified.

review_notes.scope: Full review of 218 production Apex classes (205 main package + 13 Health Check package). Excludes test classes (*Test.cls), mock classes (*Mock*.cls), interfaces (I*.cls), and enums.
methodology: Scanned all classes for WITH SECURITY_ENFORCED (found 0 - fully migrated to WITH USER_MODE)
Scanned all classes for UserInfo.getSessionId() (found 1 - ToolingApiService.cls)
Scanned all classes for Database.query without queryWithBinds (found 2 - ConfigScanEvaluator, SOQLQueryEvaluator)
Identified all @AuraEnabled methods across 93 files and verified try-catch + AuraHandledException patterns
Verified all 9 'without sharing' classes have documented security justifications
Checked all DML operations for 'as user' or AccessLevel.USER_MODE usage
Verified all inline SOQL queries use WITH USER_MODE
Checked for @future methods (found 0 - all async uses Queueable)
Verified System.debug usage is confined to logger implementations only
Validated previous review findings (Feb 2026) have been remediated

previous_review_remediation.notes: OnCallScheduleController (5 findings), MultiOrgManager (9 findings), HIPAABreachNotificationService (6 findings), ElaroDynamicReportController (2 findings), NaturalLanguageQueryService (2 findings) have all been remediated since the Feb 2026 review. The remaining 5 info-level findings from the previous review are still valid positive observations.

positive_observations: Zero instances of WITH SECURITY_ENFORCED - full migration to WITH USER_MODE complete
Zero @future methods - all async operations use Queueable pattern
All 9 'without sharing' classes have documented security justifications
18 uses of Database.queryWithBinds across the codebase (up from previous review)
ElaroScoreCallback REST endpoint is an exemplary security implementation
ElaroDynamicReportController dynamic query builder is an exemplary security implementation
All previously flagged security issues (Feb 2026) have been remediated
Consistent use of ElaroLogger structured logging instead of System.debug

standards_compliance.with_user_mode_adoption: 99% - All SOQL queries use WITH USER_MODE except Database.countQuery (which embeds it in query string)
standards_compliance.as_user_dml_adoption: 99% - All DML operations use 'as user' or AccessLevel.USER_MODE
standards_compliance.auraenabled_error_handling: 95% - 4 remaining methods need AuraHandledException (SEC-002 through SEC-005)
standards_compliance.sharing_keywords: 98% - 3 pure DTO classes missing explicit keywords (SEC-010 through SEC-012)
standards_compliance.without_sharing_documentation: 89% - 8 of 9 without-sharing classes have standard SECURITY: prefix (ElaroAuditTrailPoller needs update)
